AssessorObservation.description: Thin capture envelope for a county assessor / public-records fetch. The payload is a sparse PropertyProfile (only the sections the source provides; a valid profile still requires its `property` section); status reports the fetch outcome. By convention profile accompanies success and error accompanies failure statuses — validators deliberately do not enforce this pairing, so consumers must branch on status, not on field presence.

artifact_refs.description: References to SourceArtifact IDs in the nested profile.artifacts bundle; invalid when profile or profile.artifacts is absent.

AssessorObservation.title: AssessorObservation

Classification.description: An open-vocabulary classification qualified by the system that defines its code. Codes from different systems are not assumed equivalent.
Classification.title: Classification

CodeableConcept.description: Source coding preserved alongside canonical values (system + code + display).
CodeableConcept.title: CodeableConcept

CommercialDetails.description: Internationally neutral commercial facts. When nested in Structure, these fields describe observation-derived current state. When nested in StructureState, they are asserted historical facts governed by PropertyStateSnapshot.as_of_date. provenance.retrieved_at is retrieval metadata, not the effective date. Historical/per-period financial figures belong on dated records such as OperatingStatement and UnitRentObservation. Events reference a separately bundled PropertyStateSnapshot rather than containing the snapshot. Optional standards profiles may constrain values for a specific use case.

market_classification.description: Competitive market positioning under the named rating system; distinct from physical condition and construction quality.

parking_ratio.description: e.g. spaces per 1000 sqft — denominator explicit

tenant_count.description: Distinct legal tenants (not suites or leases); timing follows the containing Structure or StructureState context

CommercialDetails.title: CommercialDetails

id.description: Canonical identifier; nonblank with no leading or trailing whitespace

ExtractionCategory.description: What kind of property content an extraction produced. This is a content axis only — where the content came from (public record, vendor, scrape) is Provenance's job. Precedence: when the primary extracted content is a transaction, listing, or lease, use that value even if the page is also a record of property facts. `other` means successfully classified but outside this taxonomy; put the producer's raw label in source_category.
ExtractionCategory.title: ExtractionCategory

ExtractionObservation.description: Capture envelope for LLM or scrape extraction (suitable as an LLM structured-output target). The payload is a sparse PropertyProfile (only the sections the source provides; a valid profile still requires its `property` section); status reports the extraction outcome. By convention profile and category accompany success and error accompanies failure statuses — validators deliberately do not enforce this pairing (LinkML rules would materialize in only some generated validators, diverging the contracts), so consumers must branch on status, not on field presence.

artifact_refs.description: References to SourceArtifact IDs in the nested profile.artifacts bundle; invalid when profile or profile.artifacts is absent.

category.description: Content classification; expected when status is success

source_category.description: Raw or more specific producer classification, especially when category is `other`

ExtractionObservation.title: ExtractionObservation

ExtractionStatus.description: Outcome of an extraction attempt over already-fetched content. Fetch-level failures (timeout, api_error) belong to the envelope that did the fetching, e.g. AssessorStatus.
ExtractionStatus.title: ExtractionStatus

ForeclosureCase.description: A foreclosure PROCEEDING; its recorded filings append over time.

id.description: Canonical identifier; nonblank with no leading or trailing whitespace

ForeclosureCase.title: ForeclosureCase

ForeclosureCaseParty.description: Open contextual role. Mortgage and deed-of-trust proceedings commonly use lender | trustee | borrower; lien foreclosures may instead use claimant | debtor.
ForeclosureCaseParty.title: ForeclosureCaseParty

ForeclosureFiling.description: One recorded filing; postponements append as new filings.

document_number.description: Primary identifier assigned by the recording/registry authority

instrument_date.description: Date executed/signed as dated on the instrument

recorded_on.description: Date accepted, recorded, or registered by the authority

recording_authority.description: Authority maintaining the record (optional — parcel context is inference, not identity)

registry_reference.description: Alternate authority-issued reference: title, dealing, folio, or notarial-act number

status.description: nod | lis_pendens | notice_of_sale | auction_scheduled | postponement | ... (open; US-seeded)

ForeclosureFiling.title: ForeclosureFiling

InstrumentReference.description: Cross-reference to another recorded instrument (e.g., an assignment citing the original mortgage's recording number) — the target may not exist as a PHDS row.

relationship_type.description: re_records | corrects | releases | assigns | modifies | subordinates | refers_to

InstrumentReference.title: InstrumentReference

id.description: Canonical identifier; nonblank with no leading or trailing whitespace

cpi_cap.description: CPI escalation cap in percentage points; 5 means 5 percent.

cpi_floor.description: CPI escalation floor in percentage points; 2 means 2 percent.

escalation_type.description: fixed_amount | fixed_percent | cpi | step_schedule | fmv | none

escalation_value.description: For escalation_type=fixed_percent, a value from 0-100 percentage points; 3 means 3 percent. For escalation_type=fixed_amount, the increment in the currency of the parent LeaseEvent.rent and the period specified by LeaseEvent.rent_period.

LeaseEvent.description: Executed leases (not asking rents — see UnitRentObservation).

id.description: Canonical identifier; nonblank with no leading or trailing whitespace

remarks.description: Source- or vendor-authored narrative interpreted through provenance.

LeaseEvent.title: LeaseEvent

LeaseEventParty.description: role = lessee | landlord | guarantor | lessee_broker | landlord_broker
LeaseEventParty.title: LeaseEventParty

Lien.description: Involuntary encumbrances — tax, judgment, HOA, mechanic's.

document_number.description: Primary identifier assigned by the recording/registry authority

id.description: Canonical identifier; nonblank with no leading or trailing whitespace

instrument_date.description: Date executed/signed as dated on the instrument

recorded_on.description: Date accepted, recorded, or registered by the authority

recording_authority.description: Authority maintaining the record (optional — parcel context is inference, not identity)

registry_reference.description: Alternate authority-issued reference: title, dealing, folio, or notarial-act number

Lien.title: Lien

Listing.description: Listing identity and non-lifecycle facts. Process events by occurred_on ascending; array order breaks same-date ties. Carry status, asking_price, and rent_period forward independently from the latest event that supplies each field. Original asking terms come from the earliest event supplying them. close_price comes from the latest closed event supplying it.

id.description: Canonical identifier; nonblank with no leading or trailing whitespace

remarks.description: Source- or vendor-authored narrative interpreted through provenance.

Listing.title: Listing

ListingEvent.description: A dated listing assertion. asking_price is a sale price or periodic rent; rent_period states the period when asking_price is rent. close_price is asserted on a closed event.
ListingEvent.title: ListingEvent

ListingParticipant.description: role = listing_agent | co_listing_agent | buyer_agent | listing_brokerage | selling_brokerage
ListingParticipant.title: ListingParticipant

Loan.description: Recorded debt. Originating lender identity and classification live on the canonical Party referenced by a LoanParty. Assignments, modifications, satisfactions, and other lifecycle assertions are dated events; consumers derive current status and satisfaction dates.

document_number.description: Primary identifier assigned by the recording/registry authority

id.description: Canonical identifier; nonblank with no leading or trailing whitespace

instrument_date.description: Date executed/signed as dated on the instrument

interest_rate.description: Nominal interest rate in percentage points; 6.5 means 6.5 percent.

recorded_on.description: Date accepted, recorded, or registered by the authority

recording_authority.description: Authority maintaining the record (optional — parcel context is inference, not identity)

registry_reference.description: Alternate authority-issued reference: title, dealing, folio, or notarial-act number

Loan.title: Loan

LoanEvent.description: Dated loan lifecycle — assignments, modifications, satisfactions. Recording fields apply when the event is a recorded instrument; they stay null for unrecorded servicing events.

document_number.description: Primary identifier assigned by the recording/registry authority

instrument_date.description: Date executed/signed as dated on the instrument

recorded_on.description: Date accepted, recorded, or registered by the authority

recording_authority.description: Authority maintaining the record (optional — parcel context is inference, not identity)

registry_reference.description: Alternate authority-issued reference: title, dealing, folio, or notarial-act number

LoanEvent.title: LoanEvent

LoanParty.description: role = borrower | lender | beneficiary | trustee (assignees live in loan events)
LoanParty.title: LoanParty

Money.description: Monetary amount. JSON wire format is decimal-as-string. ISO-4217 currency.
Money.title: Money

OperatingStatement.description: Operating performance of a property for one period: income, vacancy, expenses, NOI. The stated totals are authoritative; line_items provide detail and are not required to sum to the totals. Canonical relations: egi = pgi - vacancy_loss (+ reimbursements where recovered); noi = egi - opex_total. capex and reserves sit below the NOI line unless the corresponding *_included_in_opex flag is true; same for ground_lease_expense. All Money fields on one statement, including line items, MUST share a single currency (validator-enforced, not expressible in LinkML). Growth-rate and other model assumptions are out of scope (valuation extension).

id.description: Canonical identifier; nonblank with no leading or trailing whitespace

noi.description: Net operating income; may be negative

period_start.description: For fiscal-year, trailing-12, or partial periods. period_start and period_end must be provided together; omit both for calendar-year statements.

statement_year.description: The calendar year the statement is for (the year containing period_end for fiscal/trailing periods).

OperatingStatement.title: OperatingStatement

OwnershipPeriod.description: One ownership regime; ended_on is end-exclusive, NULL = current.

id.description: Canonical identifier; nonblank with no leading or trailing whitespace

mailing_address.description: Owner's mailing address during THIS period

OwnershipPeriod.title: OwnershipPeriod

id.description: Canonical identifier; nonblank with no leading or trailing whitespace
Canonical identifier; nonblank with no leading or trailing whitespace

Party.description: One canonical model for every actor — owners, buyers, tenants and lessees, borrowers, lenders, brokers, trustees, claimants, contractors, associations, and valuation performers.

classifications.description: System-qualified actor classifications from an open vocabulary; use producer-namespaced system and code values

id.description: Canonical identifier; nonblank with no leading or trailing whitespace

legal_form.description: Optional system-qualified legal form for an organization under an identified jurisdictional or producer vocabulary. This is not an industry classification or a contextual role such as lender, broker, tenant, or association.

name.description: Canonical display name for this Party in the profile; nonblank with no leading or trailing whitespace. Source-specific wording is attributed through provenance or SourceArtifact.

name_first.description: Optional producer-derived parsed component of a person's canonical display name; not an independent identity and not universally applicable across naming systems.

name_last.description: Optional producer-derived parsed component of a person's canonical display name; not an independent identity and not universally applicable across naming systems.

name_middle.description: Optional producer-derived parsed component of a person's canonical display name; not an independent identity and not universally applicable across naming systems.

normalized_name.description: Producer-derived matching key for the canonical display name. Its normalization algorithm is producer-defined; it is not authoritative display text or a separate identity.

Party.title: Party

role.description: situs | entrance | alias | address_range | former | mailing | other (open vocabulary)

contractor_party.description: Canonical contractor Party reference; credential records are outside core v0.2

id.description: Canonical identifier; nonblank with no leading or trailing whitespace

kind.description: roofing | solar | hvac | adu | pool | new_construction | ... (open)

Permit.title: Permit

id.description: Canonical identifier; nonblank with no leading or trailing whitespace

PropertyParcel.title: PropertyParcel

PropertyProfile.description: The flagship interchange document: everything known about one property, as one bundle. Every section is exactly the corresponding entity shape. parties[] is the deduplicated bundle carrying every Party referenced by any record, including tenants, lenders, contractors, associations, and valuation performers (required for lossless round-trip). Conformance requires passing the entities → profile → entities round-trip test.
PropertyProfile.title: PropertyProfile

building_count.description: Producer summary when structures are not enumerated

id.description: Canonical identifier; nonblank with no leading or trailing whitespace

property_use_class.description: PUCS class when property_use_system identifies PUCS

property_use_system.description: Required when use fields are set; no default

PropertyStateSnapshot.description: Sparse asserted physical state effective on as_of_date.

basis.description: at_sale | at_listing | at_lease | inspection | reported | inferred (open vocabulary)

id.description: Canonical identifier; nonblank with no leading or trailing whitespace

PropertyStateSnapshot.title: PropertyStateSnapshot

Provenance.description: Record-level source envelope. Pure child rows (parties, areas, escalations, filings) inherit the parent envelope unless they carry their own.

confidence.description: Fraction from 0 through 1; 0.8 means 80 percent confidence.

Provenance.title: Provenance

Rating.description: A rating qualified by the system that defines its code. PHDS does not assume codes from different systems are ordinally or semantically equal.
Rating.title: Rating

RecordedInstrument.description: This row corresponds to a document recorded/registered with an authority (county recorder, land registry, notarial protocol). Narrowly about document identity and recording — legal effect lives on the host class.

document_number.description: Primary identifier assigned by the recording/registry authority

instrument_date.description: Date executed/signed as dated on the instrument

recorded_on.description: Date accepted, recorded, or registered by the authority

recording_authority.description: Authority maintaining the record (optional — parcel context is inference, not identity)

registry_reference.description: Alternate authority-issued reference: title, dealing, folio, or notarial-act number

RecordedInstrument.title: RecordedInstrument

Renovation.description: Repeatable events — never a flat year on the structure.
Renovation.title: Renovation

RentRoll.description: Dated rent and occupancy observation applicable to any property use. Header totals are authoritative reported values; lines are supporting detail and are not required to sum to the totals. All Money values on one rent roll MUST use one currency (validator-enforced). For its as_of_date, this record governs reported occupancy and rent facts; current-state fields such as Space.occupancy and CommercialDetails.occupancy_pct do not override it. A line preserves dated rent-roll assertions and does not by itself create a canonical Space, Party, or LeaseEvent.

id.description: Canonical identifier; nonblank with no leading or trailing whitespace

occupancy_pct.description: Occupancy in 0–100 percentage points; 95 means 95 percent.

RentRoll.title: RentRoll

RentRollLine.description: Supporting rent-roll detail. space resolves to canonical Space. tenant is the canonical legal lessee Party when present, and lease resolves to canonical LeaseEvent when known. When both tenant and lease are present, tenant must match a party with role: lessee declared by that lease, if the lease declares any lessee. References remain optional for aggregate, vacant, unleased, or unresolved lines; the line does not duplicate canonical space identity, tenant names, or lease dates. When a source tenant cannot be resolved to a canonical Party, omit tenant and preserve the source evidence through the RentRoll provenance and profile-level SourceArtifact records; do not mint a placeholder Party or copy the source name into extras.
RentRollLine.title: RentRollLine

ResidentialDetails.description: Internationally neutral residential facts. When nested in Structure, these fields describe observation-derived current state. When nested in StructureState, they are asserted historical facts governed by PropertyStateSnapshot.as_of_date. provenance.retrieved_at is retrieval metadata, not the effective date. Events reference a separately bundled PropertyStateSnapshot rather than containing the snapshot. Optional standards profiles may constrain values for a specific use case.
ResidentialDetails.title: ResidentialDetails

cap_rate.description: Capitalization rate in percentage points; 5.75 means 5.75 percent.

Transfer.description: Every recorded deed/instrument — the ownership ledger, NOT comps.

document_number.description: Primary identifier assigned by the recording/registry authority

effective_on.description: Legal/economic effectiveness — may differ from instrument_date and recorded_on

id.description: Canonical identifier; nonblank with no leading or trailing whitespace
